title: JAMES
body: JAMES , was missing in action the whole time, he never replied to any of my messages, i had left my personal item in the car. i left several messages to ask if I may drive by to pick up and he never replied. even when i messaged him stating i will be 30 minutes late , he never cared to respond.  Now a days there is no humans !!!!

author: Rae's Media Creation
title: I had an extremely frustrating…
body:
I had an extremely frustrating experience with Turo while trying to rent a vehicle for my anniversary. I attempted to rent a vehicle through Turo and selected Affirm as my payment option. Affirm showed that I had a scheduled payment, but Turo showed that I had no upcoming trip.

I contacted Turo support and was told there was no trip planned under my account. The problem is that no one could give me any confirmation number, cancellation number, or clear documentation because they said there was no trip. Meanwhile, Affirm still showed a payment connected to Turo. That left me stuck between Turo and Affirm with no clear answer and no rental.

After that, I still could not rent from Turo at all. I tried using a different card, a different phone number, and even a private browser window, but I still could not complete a booking. Turo could not clearly explain whether my account was restricted, whether there was a verification issue, whether it was connected to the Affirm problem, or what steps I needed to take to fix it.

This was not a casual rental. This was for a special anniversary trip, and the lack of clear communication completely ruined the experience. Turo should not allow a customer to end up with a payment issue through Affirm while showing no actual trip, then fail to explain why the customer can no longer rent.

Very disappointing customer service, poor communication, and no real resolution. I would be very cautious using Turo, especially if you are renting for an important event or using Affirm as a payment option.
reply: NA

author: J W
title: I am filing this complaint due to…
body:
I am filing this complaint due to Turo’s failure to resolve a dispute regarding a $50 promotional credit and their failure to uphold a commitment made by their support team.
On May 23, 2026, I canceled a car rental reservation for an upcoming August 2026 trip. Because this was canceled well in advance, there was no financial impact on the host or the platform. However, Turo failed to refund the $50 promotional credit applied to this booking.
When I contacted support, I was informed that the credit was forfeited and that I would essentially have to wait until next year to receive another one—a highly unreasonable response for a loyal customer of five years. I was then given escalation case number 20260523-8Q0TGW and was explicitly promised that a representative would contact me within 24 hours to discuss issuing the $50 as a travel credit.
As of May 25, 2026, no one has reached out to me, and the issue remains unresolved. I am seeking the immediate reinstatement of my $50 promotional credit. I believe that ignoring a customer's escalation case is poor business practice and violates the commitment to service that a company of Turo's size should uphold.
reply: NA

author: Melanie Bolman
title: Beware
body: The car was great, picking up dropping off no problem.Extra miles charged on drop off day Sunday. No mention of cannibis until late the next day Monday evening. Then V&G tried to say I smoked cannabis in their car. No one smokes anything, that was in their car. I replied back and they have said nothing. They are scammers  trying to get $150 more dollars from me. Don't rent from them!!!!

author: Bob Smith
title: The cars owner quoted me a price
body:
The cars owner quoted me a price, I submitted  my CC account. #s which held the car for the agreed dates. All went well with the experience until the final CC  paid receipt came and an " Airport parking fee" had been added to the negotiated price. The car was no where near an airport!
I contacted the cars owner, he said take it up with the company, TURO, and "good luck". Im in the process of just that and believe I will be credited with the backing of my credit card company and the documented invoice
When i check with TURO idiiot  AI it said somestimes this fee is "accidentally added.". Would you like to speak to my assistant. Im not kidding! 
The important thing is, REVIEW receipts that arrive weeks or months after an agreement of charges. Dispute false charges with both the rip off company, it'screp and your CC bank.
PS .dont review anything until you get ur CC statement or final receipt submitter to the CC bank.
The last word: I win. Turo loses! 
My CC bank refunded the bogus airport parking fee!

author: Kenny K
title: It's convenient and so many choices
body: It's convenient and so many choices. I also like the ability to connect with the hosts directly,if there are any mechanical issues. Not having to go through a turo 1-800 automated switchboard is a great way to keep your business streamlined

author: Juniper
title: Would give zero stars if possible
body: Would give zero stars if possible. Truly atrocious service that does not value customers at all. They have no standards for hosts so we booked two cars that consecutively got cancelled by hosts, the last being cancelled 48 hours before our trip. What they see as customer service in this situation, where using their service has put us in a terrible situation of not having a car right before we need it, is to refund your money, that you are entitled to. Avoid this company if you need a reliable service and if you need a car for a specific date or occasion or expect any decent customer service. Customer service will also just read off a script and repeat themselves. May as well be an A.I. or bot

author: Carlos Javier
title: Extremely disappointed with my recent…
body:
Extremely disappointed with my recent experience with Turo and their claims process.

During my trip, another driver backed into the vehicle, left a note, accepted responsibility, and provided insurance information. I fully cooperated from the beginning and provided everything Turo needed.

What happened next was honestly shocking.

Turo repeatedly told me the third party’s insurance “denied coverage” because the damages were supposedly below the driver’s deductible. Then, almost immediately after emailing me, they charged $500 directly to my credit card before I had any meaningful opportunity to review documents, dispute the determination, or verify the insurance information myself.

Later, the at-fault driver personally contacted Progressive again and was told there may have been a processing error. According to him, Progressive escalated the claim for payment and even stated the deductible should not apply the way Turo described it. The driver provided me with the Progressive representative’s direct contact information and encouraged Turo to contact them directly.

Despite this contradictory information, Turo chose to:

* finalize the matter internally,
* keep the charge on my account,
* and tell ME to personally recover the money from the at-fault driver myself.

That is incredibly unprofessional.

The entire process felt like an abuse of power by a company that knows it can immediately charge customers first and sort things out later. There was no meaningful dispute process, no neutral review, and no real opportunity to resolve conflicting insurance information before funds were removed from my account.

To make matters worse, the actual damage estimate was only around $681 for relatively minor bumper damage. Yet Turo acted as though the matter was completely settled while conflicting information still existed. 

I understand companies have Terms of Service. That is not the issue. The issue is how poorly and aggressively this claim was handled from a customer-service and fairness standpoint.

I expected professionalism and reasonable investigation. Instead, I got automated charges, contradictory explanations, and zero meaningful opportunity to resolve the situation before my card was charged.

Very disappointing experience.
reply: NA

author: Pierre Calbert
title: I was falsely accused of leaving…
body: I was falsely accused of leaving tobacco in a non-smoking car. It was a AI picture but because I did not take pictures myself, they still blame me. The pictures were blankly AI generated. I don’t know how far I’m gonna go with this company because if they are letting people get away with issues like this, and you cannot talk to a physical person about the situation I don’t wanna deal with this company.

author: Shamille Lockwood
title: The company needs a feature where you…
body:
The company needs a feature where you can record. I’m currently being accused of a false claim by Vince. I rented his fully loaded all power BMW SUV and soon after I picked it up he sends a message about a seat he had to get fixed prior that cost $2000. This is Vince exact text , “We had a guest break a seat while being impatient, and it cost them $2000 to have it repaired.” I knew no one was getting in the backseat while it was in my possession and I knew everything was power! 4 hours after I return he claims a back seat is broken. Vince exact text, “ Hello, one of the middle seats is stuck as it was moved without using the mechanical buttons. We have tried to recalibrate it yet. It is gonna have to be handled by a professional.
I wanted to let you know as we will have to get an estimate for the repair.” I don’t know what seat was broken prior, if it was ever fixed, or if it’s a different back seat.  While the vehicle was in my possession, no one got in the backseat. Only the front driver seat in front passenger seat was sat in. This is the message he sent 4 First and last time I’ll be using Turo. 
Vince exact text
“There was no broken seat when you picked up the car.  We sent you message telling you to use the mechanical buttons as someone previously broke a seat by not using the buttons to move the chair.  That had been repaired long aho.“

I know everything in Vince fully load SUV BMW was power. I have common sense! I didnt get in the backseat and I wouldn’t forcefully try to pull a seat down. Then Vince sent different angle pics that included others scratches/wear and tear to the interior that I never scene because I didn’t get in the backseat.

author: Pieter
title: Fantastic Customer Service
body:
Yesterday Fatllama (and more specifically Isabella) gave me the best customer service I’ve ever experienced. 

First of all, it was fast and easy to speak to a real life person and no AI bot. With some other companies (or governments) this takes ages or they make you go in circles. 

Then, Isabella acted fast and with a level of empathy and understanding I haven’t encountered before contacting a customer service. 

As a first time lender I made the mistake to refund somebody directly to their bank account. Something went wrong with the kit they hired from me and I wanted to act fast and courteous. As I refunded the whole amount (including booking fee) the payout from Fatllama to me (the total amount minus booking fee) was less then what I refunded. 
I only realised this two days after the renting period was finished and the kit was returned to me.

I explained this all to Isabella and she reassured me they could still do the full refund for the listing from Fatllama, if I was okay to ask (and trusted) the borrower to transfer back the money I send them. 

So, I received great help while it was clearly me who had made the mistake.

Hats off!
reply:
Thank you so much for your kind words and for taking the time to share your experience with us. We’re delighted to hear that Isabella provided such exceptional support and that you felt valued and understood throughout the process.

Providing fast, efficient, and empathetic customer service is something we take great pride in, so it’s truly rewarding to know that Isabella made such a positive impression. Mistakes can happen, and we’re always happy to help find the best possible solution.

We sincerely appreciate your trust in us and look forward to assisting you again in the future.

Best regards,
The Fat Llama-team

author: Shivam
title: Fat llama ALLOWS MINORS to RENT YOUR HIGH VALUE ITEMSBEWARE
body: A kid got verified by fat llama to rent my high value guitar. He made absurd requests and I got a gut feeling so I cancelled it, he then mentioned he is only 17 years old. Isn't it strange that FL is verifying under age members that have no rental history to rent out high value stuff. I contacted FL chat with my booking number but no one responded. Had he stolen my item, the police would have done nothing as he is a minor.
reply:
Thank you for sharing your concerns. We understand that trust and security are paramount when renting out high-value items, and we appreciate the opportunity to clarify our policies.

Fat Llama allows individuals aged 16 and above to rent items, but with strict limitations. Users under 18 are subject to a rental value cap and must successfully complete our verification process, which includes identity checks and other safeguards to confirm their legitimacy. As always, lenders have full discretion to decline a booking if they are uncomfortable, just as you did in this case. If any concerns arise regarding a particular borrower, we encourage lenders to reach out to our team so we can review the request further.

Regarding your support inquiry, we can see that our team did respond to your chat. We regret if you felt your concerns were not addressed in a timely manner, as we always strive to provide prompt and helpful assistance.

Kind regards,
The Fat Llama-team

author: Ed Star
title: I can only assume these reviews are…
body:
I can only assume these reviews are fake.

This is truly horrendous service, run entirely by clunky algorithm with zero customer service. Having already paid for service I have been unable to pass verification despite proving proof o f payment, official documents (passport and DL), and social media verification.

The information requested exceeds what can be reasonably requested under GDPR , and I expect this is data capture trap as much as it is peer to peer lending service. 

The complaint service is run by a chat bot, which is constitutes a serious breach of statuary/consumer rights. 

I will not only be going to financial ombsuman, but referring this to correct authority for possible data fraud.
reply:
Hi Ed,

Thank you for taking the time to leave this review.

We wanted to let you know that our unique verification system is different to most rental houses, as we do not take a cash deposit - so we manually review some higher value rentals. We're sorry you did not get approved this time round. We do have a dedicated customer service team and multiple emails you can reach out to us on if you needed help with anything. We'd love to see what we could do for you if you wanted to rent again, so please email resolutions@fatllama.com.

Best regards,

The Fat Llama team

author: Ordinary Zine
title: The customer service is awful
body: The customer service is awful, beware. No phone number - instant red flag. Prepare for all your emails to be ignored or answered by bots (or if they are people, they can’t read and consistently miss the point).  Waited over an hour for live chat and even then they replied and took 20 minutes to continue responding. They never apologised for any inconvenience and honestly it didn’t sound like a real person on the other side of the chat! Sketchy vibes.
reply:
Hi Ordinary Zine, 

I'm sorry to hear that you've experienced difficulties getting support on the platform! As a small team fielding a huge number of account verifications and queries, we are unfortunately not able to offer phone support. However, I'm happy to say that we're currently in the process of training up some additional teammates and undertaking some significant internal changes to be able to offer quicker, more thorough instant chat and email support going forward. I hope you'll give us another chance!

Best, 
The Fat Llama Team

author: Sinuhe Vazquez
title: Excellent customer service
body: They ask for a lot of information to rent the equipment, it very understandable but if you’re doing it for the first time tal your time it might take a couple of days before the approval. Although they have an excellent customer service! They’re very comprehensive and personalized, when you have a need you actually are talking to humans and not just AI I’m very happy with the experience

author: Alexandre Pierrot
title: Easy to use as a renter
body: Easy to use website as a renter. The ID verification is a great feature and allowed me to trust I was renting from a real human. The ability to message lenders before payment for a rental is great too, as you can work out the logistics of a hire before committing your money. I'll definitely keep using the platform.

author: James Fisher
title: new low bar for customer service set
body:
In an industry where time is of the essence- these guys set a new low bar for service and support. Highly not recommended
1- The have a verification process that had me alternately laughing and crying. What the f#*k is a "payslip" guys? this is an industry of contractors and freelancers, why do i need to show you my confidential business paperwork?
2- While every other rental house in the UK can verify anyone within an hour using their company reg number, a credit rating and and one or two industry references from other rental houses, these guys need two days to read my confidential invoices and tax records.
3- the verification team literally ghosted us multiple times in the days before an important shoot - neither myself nor the lender could get a response for them.
4- they have made themselves uncontactable, no phone number, their help bot only answers pre-formulated FAQ's.
5- when they do decide to email back, its a different person who knows nothing about the case, and asks for documents that you sent through 48 hours ago.
reply:
Hi James,

Thank you for taking the time to leave this review. We're sorry that this was not experience you were expecting when using us. We do sometimes require further verification for items of a higher value. Such as asking for employment documentation. We of course know that people who use us are self employed and freelancing, so we offer alternative options when providing proof of employment such as invoices etc. We do not check users credit history when they use Fat Llama, which makes our verification system unique and more accessible to users.

After having a look at this particular matter, it looks as if your emails were sent to someone at Fat Llama who no longer works for the company which is where some confusion may have come from. We also sent emails to you without a response, which may have ended up in your spam/junk folder. If there are any issues in future, please email resolutions@fatllama.com and our team will be more than happy to help.

Best regards,

The Fat Llama Team

author: Andrew Murphy
title: Everything went to plan
body: Everything went to plan! It would be good to have automatic instructions on how to collect.

author: Customer
title: My second attempt to rent was poor also...
body:
I gave a two or three star review the first time I rented. The rental went fine but I could not get the 'first time discount' to work and the 'contact support' process is terrible.

This time because the value of the item was more, they asked for proof of employment. Because I am 67 and retired the system couldn't cope. I went through various emails with customer support ( again an awful experience with long waits and simply automatic replies etc) I could not make the verification process work. The 'Tax return' option is very difficult to make work because a tax return is generally a 15 page document and no one in their right mind would share that amount of personal information. 
I have no confidence ever to use this service again. Sadly it is a great concept but the management are unable to recognise a legitimate renter and clearly clueless about the verification processes and the negative effect this can have on the brand.
reply:
Hi there,

Thank you for your time to leave this review. We're very sorry you've had this experience using the platform on this occasion. I hope the resolve we came to directly with you, was sufficient for you and that you will have the confidence to use Fat Llama again. 

Best regards,

The Fat Llama Team

author: Joe Heffernan
title: Big fees to the lender and renter
body: They charge a lot of fees to the lender and the renter. They are working both sides, why? The screening process is really unnecessary with the facial recognition. HOWEVER, my experience with the actual person lending to me was super great. So, inadvertently I can give the company maybe 3 stars. But that’s just because of my experience with the lender. To be super clear this review absolutely does not reflect upon the lender. Fat Llama: calm down with the fees on both sides.

author: cosmin popescu
title: Nasty experience
body: Nasty experience. You try to rent a thing. They ask for bank statements, passports, utility bills, take videos of yourself to prove it's you. i guess everyone is able to provide this. then they ask for social media accounts. sorry, not everyone is so social to use shady apps. i would rather pay a deposit or insurance. like no one can fake social media accounts. so silly, so stupid
reply:
Hi Cosmin,

Thank you for taking the time to leave this review.

Social media can be used to help establish someone’s identity and/or work, which helps to improve their Trustworthy Score so that we can verify the transaction. This is a standard procedure we go through with all borrowers, to make sure we go through the right security processes to protect the lenders items. You can read more about our verification process here: https://intercom.help/fat-llama/en/articles/2465076-why-am-i-being-asked-to-submit-personal-information

Your feedback has been taken on board and we hope you continue to use the platform.

Best regards,

The Fat Llama Team

author: Adeola Adeola
title: Avoid! Avoid! Avoid!
body: They have a very poor cancellation policy. It's a very drawn out process where they make it difficult for you to contact anyone (lots of automation, computer-generated messages). As a result it is a frustrating process, in the end they then refuse to give refunds despite it being clear that no rental has taken place. It is not worth the hassle. Do not bother. Save your money because this company is facilitating scammers and people pretending to offer items which they never intend to rent out. Be careful!
reply:
Hi Adeola,

Thank you for taking the time to leave this review.

It looks like this case was resolved in the end and you were given a refund. We're sorry this took longer than usual, this was because we required a case to be open so we could review this fully. You have every right as a borrower to seek compensation if you have not received an item so if you have any issues in future, please don't hesitate to get in touch.

Best regards,

The Fat Llama Team

author: Alex Grace
title: Honest lender review after over a year
body:
Going to keep it short and will write a proper detailed long review once we fully leave the platform. 

Concept is good, when we joined, the platform was great. Now, the little nuisance type issues are:

- App is buggy, slow and pretty much unusable, somehow overheats even an iphone 11 pro (they will respond by saying that they are working on improvements, but thats a lie, it has been like that for at least a year), 
- If you want to use the platform just stick your pc browser, it only bugs out in search every once in a while and in activity page when you try to move past page 1.
- If you are planning on being a lender, expect your items to depreciate, really quick (deep scratches on cameras, dust in sensors, etc etc) Fatllama wont cover it, its wear and tear (read their T&Cs) . 
- 4 out of 5 borrowers will be late to return the kit, they are only on time to collect. 
- When borrowing, people will be rude, people will pop kit into ubers when they cannot be asked to return themselves and not tell you (uber does not have the appropriate insurance btw), they will lose cables and little accessories (doesn't sound like an issue but when replacements cost £10 and it happens once a week, its just an additional cost that fatllama will do absolutely nothing about).
- If something gets broken, you better have video proof just before the rental, because once again, borrowers will just try to say that it was given to them that way. If you can't prove it, fatllama will not do anything.

Now onto the major issues:
- FEES, they've recently increased their fees to 25% each way to both borrower and lender, so if you list something for £100 per day, you get £75, borrower pays £125 (+ getting their own hire out insurnace) and fatllama make £50. They will respond with a link to a blog explaining why they've increased the fees, but as all lenders will agree, changing a line of code to amend how weekly discounting works, does not warrant a 10% fee increase each way. It would be understandable if they actually added features to the platform or made it easier to contact them (they do the opposite of that one, chatbot does everything possible to get you to email them rather than speak to a human being). 

- As mentioned above, kit gets broken, A LOT

- Stuff gets stolen, also A LOT, I want to bring examples from my own and other lender experiences (we all speak to each other) but would rather stay anonymous until the American big daddy competitor gets here near the end of the year. Not going to call them by their name but its a grid to share of sorts. Not gonna get into it, will probably get the review removed.  

- Insurance for lenders is a 50/50, sometimes you get paid, sometimes you dont. Late return fee payments are just like betting on 0 in a roulette wheel, they will try to charge the borrowers bank account once and then pass it onto their security team, which means you will not see the late return fee money.

Anyway, I got bored of trying to not use examples to stay anonymous, will post a full one once we leave the platform in full.
reply:
Hi Alex,

Thank you for taking the time to leave this review.

We are sorry you feel this way about the platform after so long, wanted to address some of the issues you have been facing.

- Regarding the app, our product team work hard on fixing the bugs that have been flagged to us. These types of fixes come every few and we hope the next release will have a better usability for you and other users
- Regarding the fee increases, our intention here is not financial gain, however, in order for the business to continue to evolve, we need to make these changes. As mentioned above, we have a number of things we are working on as well as fixing bugs and making the user experience smooth and seamless. Now this is no quick fix, and we greatly appreciate your patience in this time whilst we work on further developments
- In regards to the Lender Guarantee, we do have certain criteria that needs to be met for a lender to be covered. For example, as you mentioned, taking pre rental videos and post rental videos which you can see all of the criteria in our FAQ's. For every case reported, our Resolutions team will do their best to resolve in the fairest way possible.

We thank you for being a loyal customer and hope we can improve our services to your satisfaction so you stay with us. 

If you have any further feedback or concerns, please email resolutions@fatllama.com and we would be happy to discuss.

Best regards,

The Fat Llama Team

author: Aissa Laribi
title: Trustworthy score in question(updated review)
body:
After having sending all the documents, I have been refused to have access to fatllama because their algorithm consider my profile at risk of non payment, despite not having negative credit score, or previous unpaid bills or loans. I found this experience frustrating and insulting.

Update from July 2020, after the review I have been able to use the service. And overall, the experience was enjoyable.
reply:
Hi Aissa,

Thank you for your feedback on this.

Our resolutions team have had a good look into this regarding wrongful deletion, we've emailed you and hope you will choose to use Fat Llama again.

Please have a look out for our email.

Best regards,

The Fat Llama Team

author: Sixto Joost
title: Well, here's what happened to me
body: Well, somebody should have told me how to actually complete the rental. I chose an RV, input my dates, received a cost summary but couldn't find a way to actually make the deal. Tried dealing with the WORST AI experience I've ever had (and AI is ra rarely a good experience but dont get me started). After an eternity of trying to make the deal, I was offered a phone number. I called it and met a nice guy who helped me navigate the peculiar procedure used by this company. I had to "register" but was never told this on the website; I was directed to messaging the owner who never messaged me back. I put my phone number in the message bc I wanted to arrange to SEE the vehicle in person before I committed 4000 dollars. Two things: at this point I was creating a process based on many experiences of buying and renting stuff on the internet and doing it without any direction from Outdoorsy. Well, somehow I failed.  Then, I got an email: "you haven't completed rental...."  Against my better judgment, I took the bait. I was offered AI disguised with a human name. Finally, I know what AI is going to do; it's going to make murderers of us all.  Now, after hours of effort, I still have my money but no RV.  Anyone out there willing to rent an RV to a retired attorney with the highest credit rating available, money to burn,and passionately wanting to experience RVing for a couple of weeks?  I'm your guy.
reply:
Hi there,

I'm very sorry to hear you had trouble completing your reservation and that your experience with our chat service did not provide the level of support you were looking for. While our AI is designed to handle quick requests, it is certainly not meant to replace human support. If you find that the AI is not meeting your needs, simply type "live agent" or "representative" in the chat, and you will be connected to a specialist immediately.

Additionally, I'd like to reassure you that our 24/7 Customer Support number, 877-723-7232, is clearly indicated on every page of our website to ensure you can always reach our live support team directly.

I'd love the opportunity to learn more about your experience; however, I'm unable to locate your account with the information available here. Please contact me directly at experience@outdoorsy.com with "ATTN: DALE" in the subject line, and I'll be more than happy to assist you with your booking personally.

I look forward to connecting with you. Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback.

Outdoorsy Support

author: James Krickeberg
title: Hosts can cancel at last minute without…
body: Hosts can cancel at last minute without penalty leaving buyers in a terrible position. Customer service is an AI bot. Awful. Will never use again after this rental.
reply:
Hi James,

We’re really sorry you had to deal with your original booking being canceled, we know that’s never a great experience and can throw a wrench into your plans. While we do everything we can to prevent cancellations, there are times when something unexpected comes up, like in this case where the host’s tow vehicle needed repairs after a prior rental. We completely understand how frustrating that can feel, especially when you’re counting on everything going smoothly. That’s exactly why we have a team dedicated to helping guests through last-minute changes and getting things back on track as quickly as possible. We also take cancellations seriously on the host side, and they can face impacts to their rating along with potential financial penalties.

That said, we’re really glad you were able to find a replacement so quickly, that’s always a relief in situations like this. As for the chat experience, our AI is there to help with quick questions, but it’s definitely not meant to replace a real person. If it ever feels like it’s not getting you where you need to go, just type “live agent” or “representative” and you’ll be connected right away. You can also call our Customer Support team at 877-723-7232 anytime, we’re here 24/7 and always happy to help.

Thanks so much for taking the time to share your experience with us, we really do appreciate it.

Outdoorsy Support

author: Matt B.
title: Platform - Great / Roadside Service - Needs Help
body:
The platform has been great, however I was recently hosting a trip that had an issue in Southern Utah - a heavily traveled area with National Parks - and there was no mobile mechanic available to help diagnose an issue my renter was having as no one has the Mercedez Benz diagnostic tools. 

Considering a majority of Class B rentals on the site are MB Sprinters, I found this very concerning about future rentals and the help that is available to my renters.

author: Darlene
title: If you're a host considering Outdoorsy
body:
If you're a host considering Outdoorsy, proceed with caution. I had an account previously and decided to return—big mistake. I was prepared to resubmit everything from scratch, which would’ve been fine. But because my original email was no longer valid, I couldn’t reset my password or create a new setup with that email. That took a full day to resolve.

Then came the real headache. After spending two hours setting up my listing again, I hit a wall with the W-9 form—it wouldn’t submit. That issue still isn’t fixed after 11 days, despite two emails claiming it was. I’ve spent hours emailing, calling, uploading print screens, and getting nowhere.

Customer service is polite but powerless. You can’t speak to anyone who can actually fix the problem. Everything is “escalated” to the technical or tax team, but no one follows up. Apparently, the tax team doesn’t work weekends… and I'm beginning to wonder if they work during the week either. Customer service reps even admit they’re frustrated by the escalation delays.

Outdoorsy's site claims a 24–48 hour response window, but that seems more like wishful thinking. I haven’t heard a thing in four days.

It’s now June in Maine—peak RV rental season—and I’m losing valuable time and income because of their broken system. I've switched to RVShare out of desperation. I’m not sure if it’s much better, but I can't waste another minute dealing with Outdoorsy's dysfunction.

For a platform that takes a 25% cut, they ought to provide actual support. Sadly, I’ve since found I’m not the only one with these issues—if you look up reviews, you’ll see plenty of similar stories. Outdoorsy really needs to get its act together.
reply:
Hi Darlene,

I'm very sorry to hear you've had a less-than-perfect experience with our service due to the delay in resolving the issue with your tax information on your Outdoorsy account. I know how important it is for you to get this resolved immediately, and we definitely don't want you missing out on potential bookings for the peak RV rental season, so I'd like to reassure you that we've followed up with our Tax team and made your case a priority. I'll personally get in touch with you soon regarding this. Thank you for taking a moment to provide us with your feedback. 

Dale R.
Outdoorsy Support

author: Chris Kutensky
title: I have been a long time host and renter, with Outdoorsy and others
body:
I have been a long time renter and host, for 8yrs with Outdoorsy, as I recall. I'm my experience, they have a fantastic website and process. At days end, they are middle men. I felt that they handled CV19 when business was ten fold, smoothly when they were likely under staffed and many inexperienced renters, we're on the road renting RVs because airplanes were shutdown. I host in CO, a tourist state and if I had 20 Airstreams, I could have rented them all, easily. I can only imagine how hard Outdoorsy and other RV vendors worked to try to keep millions of units and millions of personalities Happy! It's a law of averages, and you'll always have some negatives, but as you can see it's 5% and when I was a kid, 95% was an A+.  Having rented and hosted for years I've learned any negative review is just like anything, it's based on personality and expectations, unless there is a blatant neglect of communication. Otherwise, personality plays a role, and I'm just as guilty of having too high expectations and no different than a dating website, every profile has a certain effort to market, whether price point, or allowing Pets when other do not.
So, I say, always look at the big picture and the % of positive stars as some people post negatively based on emotion, or one-sided points of views, for example, host or renter. I recommend you consider the RVs age first, as age has wear/tear, and match that age to the competitive cost. Photos are great on sewing the design and general unit, but it doesn't show parts and like an aged car, truck, computer, what have you, age plays a role. Personally, I am thankful for Outdoors, introducing me to some fantastic, renters and I truly enjoy RVs and love being a Host, seeing renters come home after their journey and family vacation, all relaxed and happy. You can see the stress that has left their shoulders from PickUp to DropOff. BUT that said, there will always be certain personalities that you just can't live up to their expectations, and as a Host, you just tell them, text me, call me, let me know how I can help. We will handle any issues that comes up, if any, work with RV repair vendors, pay them, and upon drop off, if determined it wasn't Wear/Tear, we have deposits to reimburse us, but we just want you to enjoy, and so does Outdoors. It's a vacation, time to relax and enjoy and avoid making Mountains out of MoleHills as they say, as it's all comes out in the wash.  And over many years, I have issues as both renter and host, always minor but even issues with Brand New trucks or Rvs, it can happen, so keep comms open, that's what I recommend. No one wants a bad vacation for anyone, and that certainly not why Outdoorsy and Hosts option this industry. I saw this as I've had 1 or 2 personalities who tend, finding to complain whether RV, other industries / business they get involved in, they just tend to demand and it's just characteristic, no matter who the vendor may be. In today's world, it's hard to connect with this persona as they communicate one-way, and talk at you, instead of with you. I've seen very very few, but it's exaggeration and characteristic of the submitter, but to submit 1 star, even start to a middleman vendor, seems more emotional, than offering readers the positives. Why blame Outdoorsy unless the entire vacation was unruly managed start to finish, canceled or destroyed, at their direct blame. I just think emotions are not good reviews. With a great of Tv experience, I know some folks get upset about small things where others accept that "life happens". Wear and tear exists when you rent units over 5yrs, it's part of Rv life, there are tons of forums you should look into, per model you wish to rent, as that can help too.  Life is short, enjoy your vacation and just know that the older the "rental unit" it's more likely to have some small glitch occur, and don't try to fix or work on a Hosts Rv, without working with them, as I've seen people break things trying to Google How To, so let your Host handle it. And if an RV repairman need to get involved, hand it off to Host, they need to know who's working on their unit, and they know their Rv best, or either way, let them pay direct, hand it off to them, so you can go back to enjoying your time with family, friends or your journey.
Two way communication is the answer. As long as there is two-way communication, there is no issue that can't be resolved. And no need to blame Outdoorsy or other vendors in general, your our Guest(s). Just work together and you will be in the 95%, I say, from my years in Rv works. Hope this helps!

author: kerriecameron
title: At a loss for our holidays because customer service did nothing to help us
body:
After booking an RV we tried contacting the host to set up arrangements.  The host wasn't responding. I emailed host once 3 weeks and a second time one week before reaching out to customer support for help.  

Customer supports response was that they give hosts 24-48 hours to respond because "we understand life can throw your routine off".  I replied with our timeline of trying to contact the host and asked that they help us reach the host. I haven't heard anything back from customer support after this. 

I emailed multiple times and I called multiple times. No one would answer the call, and once I was on hold for 30 minutes (no exaggeration) to only be disconnected after a recording told me no one was available to take my call. 

I reached out on social media with my situation and got a response to call or email customer support for assistance... really?!  That response just added more to my frustration.

I had to cancel my reservation one week before our holidays because of the unresponsive host and lack of support from the folks at outdoorsy.  Thankfully the amount was deposited back into my account.  

Now aside from this frustrating experience with outdoorsy and their host, I am $80 out of pocket for the "service fee" that wasn't refunded... Really?!  What service did I pay for??  A whole lot of wasted time on my end for trying to get service, I should charge outdoorsy for my time, not the other way around. 

But the worst part of all this is my family and I have been looking forward to this, our annual holiday that we work very hard to afford and now we don't know what we're going to do.  We have a campground  booked with nothing to bring with us. So to quote your customer service I guess "life can throw your routine off" but replace "life" with "outdoorsy".

From this experience, my take away is outdoorsy doesn't care. At no point did anyone on your end try to resolve this matter.  You have profited off my service fee and my family is now at a loss on what we will do.  An appropriate action on your part would have been to 1) Respond to our concerns first and foremost 2) Try to resolve our concerns by helping us contact the host 3) If the host remained unresponsive, find another solution if possible.  Would there have been another rental you could have helped us manage? We even would have paid more for a bigger camper if necessary.  But unfortunately none of that happened.

No one wins from this experience! Outdoorsy, sure you made money from my service fee, but you had to refund $800 and our holidays are one week away and we don't know what we're going to do.

Outdoorsy if you do care about the people you are providing services to and making a profit off of, you still have time to make this right for me and my family. I have little hope, but at this time I have little options.
reply:
Hi Kerrie, 
 
I'm so sorry your host was unresponsive and you ended up needing to cancel your reservation. This is most definitely not the intended outcome we'd prefer our guests to have, especially so close to your trip starting. Due to the nature of the cancellation, we've refunded you in full, and our replacement team has worked to locate an alternate RV for your trip. Please feel free to respond directly to their email to let them know how you'd like to proceed. In the meantime, I'll follow up with you personally to ensure we've done everything we can to turn your experience around. Your time is extremely valuable, so thank you for taking a moment to share your feedback.
 
Jaime
Outdoorsy Support

author: Anita
title: The host scammed us
body: Booking an RV through Outdoorsy was straightforward, but our trip was plagued with issues that led us to request a refund. Unfortunately, Outdoorsy was unable to resolve the matter with the host and redirected us to a third party. Since January 2024, the host owes us $2,000 in compensation, yet Outdoorsy has not taken responsibility. Even worse, the host continues to rent out the RV. Despite numerous calls, I’m always told, 'We will contact you in 48 hours,' but no one ever follows up. This level of service is unacceptable. Save yourself the hassle and book elsewhere!
reply:
Hi Anita, 
 
I'm so sorry to hear that you had a poor experience with your trip and are dissatisfied with the outcome of a dispute. This is most definitely not the intended experience we'd prefer our guests to have. I'd love the opportunity to investigate this further, but unfortunately I'm unable to locate an account using the information provided here. Please feel free to email me directly at experience@outdoorsy.com with "Attn: Jaime" as the subject, and I'll be glad to look into what happened here. Your time is valuable, so thank you for taking a moment to share your feedback with us. I look forward to connecting with you.
 
Jaime
Outdoorsy Support

author: Stephanie Peoples
title: JUST STAY AWAY FROM OUTDOORSY-READ THE REVIEWS AND THE FINE PRINT
body:
JUST STAY AWAY FROM OUTDOORSY! 
Today is 7/29/24 and I am still dealing with a bogus scam claim from 7/4/24, 25 days later!  I have spent several hours over many many days trying to communicate with Outdoorsy and I deeply regret using them. 

We are experienced campers.  We decided to rent an RV this time to make things easy (ha!).  We paid about $400 (in addition to rental, insurance, and Outdoorsy fees) to have it delivered and set up. During our rental over the 4th of July, the power cord + surge protector failed...resulting in a smoking, melted plug.  We were left with no power, no A/C, and we are in Texas in the middle of Summer. To our surprise, the the owner filed a damage claim against us which resulted in many headaches and countless hours of wasted time-with no real help from Outdoorsy. In fact, whenever I called, I could only speak with someone in the PHILIPPINES-never the same person, and never a last name.  Sometimes someone would respond via email, but would not be available by phone.  There was always a promise to escalate, but no follow-through.

We had actually purchased the stationary insurance (also called excess insurance), which we learned that they defer to YOUR PERSONAL AUTO POLICY FIRST.  When I went back, I eventually found this in the fine print, but had to click through several pages and links to get to-essentially a rabbit hole. After the owner was told by our insurance company that this was not a qualified claim (and subsequently denied), he then pursued it with the Outdoorsy stationary policy-which was also denied. He told our insurance company that he would pursue it through the security deposit. They then emailed to ask us to approve a charge of $400-to pay for their faulty equipment-even after 2 denials stating that we were not responsible for their equipment failing. We declined. We cannot get anyone at Outdoorsy to help us with this, though we have reached out via phone and email multiple times. Today we learned that Outdoorsy has charged our card for ANOTHER $750-the original amount of our deposit.

After another call and another request to escalate-they are telling us that they have released it.  I have yet to confirm that with my CC company, but will need to make sure that all future attempts to charge us will be denied.  

A side note of the stationary/excess policy-they also add an additional charge for roadside assistance-which you should not need if you are not towing the vehicle.  In fact, the policy becomes null and void if you move the camper for any reason on a stationary policy.  

We would never even think of trusting Outdoorsy again-it's really just a platform, taking a cut, and cheaply outsourcing their customer service. 

There is no protection and no one to call who can do anything to help.
reply:
Hi Stephanie,
 
I'm very sorry to hear you've had a less-than-perfect experience with our platform due to a damage claim. It's our goal to provide a smooth and easy claims experience, so thank you for taking a moment to provide us with your feedback. Transparency is vitally important to us, so we'll be performing a full investigation into what happened here and will be reaching out to you soon to address your concerns. I look forward to connecting with you. 
 
Jaime
Outdoorsy Support

author: Nigel Bell
title: Abandoned by Outdoorsy
body:
I hired a RV through Outdoorsy through August 2023 (20 nights). They sub-let to another company in the days/week before commencement - being told the campervan I booked (5 months earlier) was no longer available. A 'similar' vehicle was available when I arrived (same price, same conditions).  Wrong.
No stealth camping as few curtains (!), no pillows (!), limited stove or utensils (!), no outdoor seating or  cover (!).  Not what I expected or paid for ....

But worse was the developing problems with the two-year old automatic vehicle (a Mercedes van!).
From Day 1 it had problems - wrong gear, shuddering forward, over-revving - which was increasingly dangerous when driving on US freeways at half the set speed.  Stop-start - stop - shudder - stop - start - stop altogether - so multiple calls to the intermediary company, then Outdoorsy.

Outdoorsy provides intermittent phone calls/call-backs during the afternoon, leading to a request to drive (!) to the nearest specialist repair workshop (30 miles away).  Tried on back-roads .. but same problems and the vehicle again died half way, leaving me at the edge of the road in 108F degree heat in the middle of no-where ... for 15 hours /overnight!   
  
Many Outdoorsy phone calls - no action - before next morning the tow vehicle was sent to the previous (wrong) address!

Eventually, towed away to the repair shop.  Long wait before they diagnosed the problem.  Meantime, Outdoorsy told me I was one my own - they wouldn't supply an alternative vehicle.  I was expected to start all over again with a new hire (whilst marooned in the middle of nowhere!).  
"Not good enough, we have a paid contract" I said - and let me speak to the manager.  It took a week before a manager sent an email back, asking about my problem !!!!   

And fortunately for me, the dealer found and fixed the 'drive' cables/electronics that had been chewed through by 'varmints' prior to my hire.  So after two delayed days of hassle I was able to continue.

LESSONS:  
Outdoorsy would not meet their contractual conditions when it got hard....they made their vehicle replacement problems mine ...
Outdoorsy is better at PR and marketing than delivering ...   
Outdoorsy subsequently made it very difficult to get a refund for the lost two days of travel ... 
Don't believe their promotional spin .. my experience says you're on you're own if there's a vehicle replacement problem.
reply:
Hi Nigel, 
 
I'm so sorry to hear you had a poor experience with your trip and the outcome of your resolution. This is certainly not the intended experience we'd prefer our guests to have. After further review of your account, I can confirm I found no violation of our contractual obligations and your requested refund during the dispute resolution process was transacted back to your payment method on 9/20/23. Your time is valuable, so thank you for taking a moment to share this feedback with us. I'll be reaching out to you directly to discuss this further.
 
Jaime
Outdoorsy Support

author: Brendan Forrest
title: Absolute NIGHTMARE Story
body:
Hired an RV out of LA by Eugene. A day before hire ended, the RV broke down in 50 degree C heat. And the Host actions were appalling, Constantly blaming us and making us stay with the RV even though the location of brake down was not safe. We purchased the highest level of insurance through outdoorsy, and at this point found out that the insurance means nothing. The is no "Concierge" service as stated. They call a tow truck for you and thats about it. No alternate hotel room or ride. The host then took us to disputes in house at outdoorsy and they were completely toothless, resolved nothing and only Liaised between us and the Host. They then sent us to Fair clams where the host sued us for $23,000 in damages allegedly cause by us. Long story short, he lost and now won't give us our deposit back when he needs no evidence of wrong doing to claim against his customers. 

In summation. Insurance means nothing, 
when on the road no one is obligated to assist you and if something goes wrong they pass you further down the line till they can wipe their hands of you. Spend the extra money and use a reputable business that actually maintain their vehicles.
reply:
Hi Brendan, 
 
I'm terribly sorry to hear your trip ended with a breakdown dispute, and that you're dissatisfied with our resolution process. This is most definitely not the intended experience we'd prefer our guests to have. I'll be looking into this further and reach out to you directly to discuss this in greater detail. Your time is valuable, so thank you for taking a moment to share your feedback with us. 
 
Jaime
Outdoorsy Support

author: D. Mia
title: Outdoorsy's insurance is a scam.
body: Outdoorsy's insurance is at best misleading, at worst a scam.  When you read the details, their $1,000,000 insurance coverage claim is for DAMAGE to an RV, not Liability Coverage like normal.  You'll never need the $1,000,000 Damage coverage because no one is renting a $1,000,000 RV.  Their liability coverage is actually just the legal minimum, often just $20,000 or so, which is nothing for liability coverage.  And it's overpriced.
reply:
Hi there, 

I'm so sorry to hear you're dissatisfied with our protection packages. Thank you for taking a moment to share your feedback with us. Please allow me to clarify the insurance a bit more. We partner with Assurant Mutual, and all protection packages will vary depending on the Class, year, make, and model of the RV, and those rates are set at industry standards based on the Actual Cash Value of the RV provided to us by the host. This coverage is required for RVs that are being delivered as well. Similar to an auto rental, RVs must have this episodic coverage to protect both parties should any vandalism, theft, acts of nature, etc. occur while the RV is in your possession.

For more information about our coverages, please visit https://www.outdoorsy.com/help/owners-protection-packages.

Jaime
Outdoorsy Support

author: Sarah Purington
title: Best Customer Support - how unusual!
body:
I have finished my 3rd rental recently (as a host), and I could not be more pleased. I have needed to utilize Outdoorsy Support numerous times as I was learning to navigate their site, app, financial questions, and how to properly prepare for renting my RV out - probably between 10-15 times. Each time, I felt that I was talking to a real person who attentively listened to my problem and found a solution promptly.
Every single time, the person I was communicating with found a solution - without having to transfer me, put me on a significant hold, or otherwise make me jump through hoops. Outdoorsy has some of the best support I have ever experienced, both on the phone and on their Chat.

I had a lot of worries when I first endeavored to list my RV on a rental platform, and subject it to the treatment of complete strangers. But I have been pleasantly surprised at my experience thus far - my renters have been excellent communicators, attentive listeners when I walk them through my rv, and have evidently treated my RV as if it was their own, prized possession. 

I highly recommend Outdoorsy as a trusted platform to offer personal RVs for rent. As a host, I find their services to be well worth the commission. I sincerely hope they maintain their best quality, which certainly gives them an extreme edge in the market - which is their customer support.

author: Iyanna
title: It’s wonderful to speak to an actual…
body: It’s wonderful to speak to an actual human being when you or your renter call in for assistance! Excellent communication, prompt response, and very helpful answering questions/troubleshooting! Will continue to use!

author: Dr Faustus
title: Nightmare registering driving license
body:
If you try to register tour driving license and you're unlucky, the automatic system will direct you to manual registration. This resulted in my case in an endless e-mail thread with different adresses , robots and people, long phonecalls woth the customer service, wich has no direct acess to your profile or documents. They will make you shooting selfies with your passport in your hand and send you from a to b, trying to catch 22.
It is more than a week since i started the process and in one week from now I reserved a car...
I rent cars regularly for business and leisure.
This is the most difficult und user unfriendly process I ever experienced.
reply:
Hi Dr. Faustus,

I'm sincerely sorry to hear you had a less-than-perfect experience with our driver verification process. This is certainly not the experience we prefer our guests to have. I'd love the opportunity to see what happened here and ensure that you're all set on your upcoming booking; however, I'm unable to locate your account with the information available. Please contact me directly at experience@outdoorsy.com with "ATTN: DALE" in the subject line. I look forward to connecting with you soon. Thanks for your feedback.

Dale R.
Outdoorsy Support

author: JP - Meli Cosentino
title: Not a fan of the billing breakdown
body:
Not a fan of the billing breakdown, or how I was shorted several times from a pay out. 

Not a fan of the algorithm in order to keep my rig relevant and findable.
reply:
Hi JP and Meli,

I'm so sorry to hear you're dissatisfied with our platform. This is most definitely not the intended experience we'd prefer our hosts to have. I'll be investigating this further on my end, and I'll reach out to you directly to address your concerns. Thank you so much for taking a moment to share your feedback with us.

Jaime
Outdoorsy Support

author: Scott Federman
title: Not good for RV owners
body: The app does not work well. They give my location without someone renting. Customer service can take a long time and often they do not understand me. It seems to be a language barrier. Worst of all is that I canceled a renter the day before his rental and Outdoorsy placed a negative review on me. There were circumstances that caused the cancelation. As a business partner I was surprised that they choose to give me a bad review. I called them to discuss it and was told that it is automatically generated by the computer, and they cannot do anything about it. This might be the beginning of AI taking over. They also charge a service fee to the owner to collect fees like fuel and things that are missing. I recommend using a different platform to rent out an RV.
reply:
Hi Scott,

I'm sorry to hear your experience with our service didn't match your expectations. This is certainly not the experience we prefer our members to have. Please allow me to clarify that an automated review noting a cancellation will not affect the host's overall rating. A host can respond to the review if they wish to clarify the cancellation. I'll personally be reaching out to you soon to provide you with more information regarding our cancellation policy and to learn more about your experience with our mobile app. Thank you for sharing your feedback.

Dale R.
Outdoorsy Support

author: MATTRESS CLEARANCE CENTER OF N
title: Warranty people  do not respond no one…
body: Warranty people  do not respond no one helps me

author: Cabin Wheels
title: Easy to use, help if you need it…
body: This is an easy platform for connecting rv owners and renters.  Simple to use, and when there is an issue they are helpful- always a real human to answer your call.

author: FL from SC PA
title: Watch your reviews and communicate quickly if there is an error.
body: UPDATE:  As can happen with technology - a problem can arise that was unintended and that appears to be what happened to me.  For that reason, I am amending my comments because while the system is flawed - the customer service team responded professionally, acknowledged a system flaw, and confirmed that it shouldn't have happened - which is GREAT.  Mistakes happen, and that is understandable. I feel confident that my issue was a glitch and for that reason, and the great communication with Outoorsey, I am revising my star rating from 1 to 4.  PRIOR REVIEW:  I saw an "automated review" on my account today that said, "Faith Lucchesi canceled reservation two days before our trip. Automated review."  While true in part, the guest initiated the cancellation, and to be kind, I AGREED TO REFUND ALL FEES that would have been due because of the cancellation policy. By agreeing to assist a renter, Outdoorsy's automated system told all other renters that I canceled someone's trip without the added fact that I did it at their request and agreed to refund all monies despite the cancellation policy. When I saw the review, many months after the harm was done, I called to discuss the matter, and the review was removed, but the damage was done. I was seriously misportrayed by the platform for more than six months, without my knowledge -and it was THEIR automatic response to the situation that published the review. NOT the guest, not me as host. And it was a lie as stated. The corporate decision to punish hosts who help guests is a shockingly myopic business decision and others should know this when reading reviews or considering becoming a host.
reply:
Hi Faith,

I'm so sorry to hear you were penalized due to a mutual cancellation. This is most definitely not what should've happened, and I'll be investigating on my end to find out why this occurred. We absolutely don't intend to punish hosts for providing their guests with a full refund, so your review is quite concerning to me. Your time is valuable, so thank you so much for sharing your feedback and bringing this to my attention. I'll be reaching out to you directly to provide insight on how this unfortunate situation may be prevented in the future. 

Jaime
Outdoorsy Support

author: Phill
title: Sharing my great experience with Kyle. Ambasador of the RVShare brand.
body:
I would like to share my great experience with Kyle with the RVShare leadership team. 

This is our first time renting a travel trailer and I had questions after I booked the reservation. Kyle did an outstanding job patiently walking me through the answers to all my questions and details of the program in a way that was easy for me to understand. 

Your decision to keep a direct phone number and ability to talk to a real person is a company differentiator. Keep that as part of your brand promise. 

I will most likely rent again from RVShare as a result of my positive experience with Kyle.  Hopefully when it's time we can work with Kyle again. Kyle is an ambassador of the RVShare brand. 

Thank you Kyle. 
Phill
reply: Awesome to hear, Phill! Thank you!

author: Matt Ziebart
title: Ruined our family vacation
body:
I paid extra for RVshare’s cancellation protection insurance to cover us if anything went wrong. Despite this, RVshare canceled our RV rental at the last minute—after we had taken time off work and planned our entire family vacation. By the time they notified us, it was impossible to find another RV rental.

RVshare claimed the owner canceled the trip. I asked for compensation as promised in the cancellation coverage policy. Instead, I spent hours on hold with their overseas customer service, was bounced around, hung up on, and called back—only to be offered a pathetic $25 promo code for my next trip.

RVshare customer service is a nightmare: an endless phone tree loop, nearly impossible to reach a real person. This feels like an online company with almost no real staff. If you’re looking for a reliable RV rental, avoid RVshare—your trip, time, and money are too important to risk on a company that doesn’t honor its own cancellation policy.

author: Curt Altarac
title: Beware of Hidden Fees and Poor Customer Service
body:
I attempted to rent an RV through RVshare, but after checkout I received an email informing me of several unexpected insurance fees—none of which were clearly disclosed upfront, and there was no way to opt out. I tried contacting customer service immediately, but no one answered. Days later, I finally spoke with someone who clearly had handled this type of complaint many times before. They walked me through the cancellation process—but still kept the “service fee” despite the misleading experience.

I’ve rented RVs, homes, and more over the years, and this was by far the strangest and least transparent experience I’ve had. I ended up canceling and losing money, but I felt compelled to leave a review so others are aware. There are better, more honest local companies out there—do yourself a favor and avoid RVshare.

author: Tricia Sake
title: Terrible costumer service
body:
Terrible costumer service! The lady on the claim never would answer her phone. If we were lucky, she would email us a short and sweet message! When we did actually talk to her, she ended up hanging up on us! She ended up closing the case without talking to us and hearing our side. 
We got an email saying we were getting the security deposit back then all of a sudden we werent! So now we are out $1500 because the guy scammed us and rvshare didn't do their job nor did the case work give a crap!! Please do yourself a favor and look somewhere else to rent a camper! Such a joke of a company

👇🏿⬇️👇🏿 They have an automatic response to your message acting like they care. They truely don't care. We have been trying to get this resolved for a couple weeks and haven't got anywhere! Seriously please look elsewhere
reply: Hi Tricia, we're so sorry to hear about your experience. We’re happy to look into this issue for you. Please reach us at 1-888-482-0234, chat with us at rvshare.com, or email us here https://help-rvshare.force.com/s/contactsupport.

author: Jennifer Velitchkov
title: I would give no stars
body: I would give no stars, but like everything else with this company, you have to comply or you cannot proceed.  I made a reservation, was informed after I paid my deposit that I had to get insurance.  I contacted the site to inform them and they told me I had to choose their insurance.  Their insurance did not correspond to my rental and is ridiculously expensive.  When I asked for a manager I was told that no one would help me and that the 'terms say .....'.  I then asked to cancel and began the process only to find out they wanted me to agree to a $60.05 service charge to cancel.  I couldn't proceed with the cancellation unless I agreed.  The worst experience ever.  What a waste of time.
reply:
Hi Jennifer, we're sorry to hear about your experience. Every RV rental is required to secure RVshare Insurance and Protection in case anything accidental happens while the RV is on a trip. This also includes trips where the RV might be delivered or stationary for the duration of the trip. Even if the RV is delivered or stationary, damages may still occur, examples include fire, acts of nature like storms and falling trees, etc.

Coverage during the rental period will vary depending on the package that is selected during checkout. For more information on what the package includes visit the Insurance and Protection page at https://rvshare.com/insurance

author: Sandra Goswick
title: Morris was super helpful
body:
Morris was super helpful! Solved a problem no one else was willing to take the time to do. 
Thanks so much!

author: Alex
title: Paid and Website is unable to complete the Reservation
body:
I tried to rent a RV from them, filled out all the info, and paid the money via credit card.  I got back a few minutes latter an email that my ID check failed.  I emailed and called for help, but all they could say is keep trying.  Well the webpage and links they sent to verify kept showing up as a blank screen.  I tried multiple browsers and PC's with no luck.  I used Chrome to see there were HTTP 500 Internal Server errors and sent screen shots, but no one helped to seemed to contact their own IT department.

It makes me feel they created this website to take your money and make you so frustrated that you cancel the reservation yourself and then loose a portion of payment.

Update: 30-Dec-2023.  After conversations with a manager on the phone, it was clear there was no simple solution on getting my data uploaded for verification.  I asked for a 100% refund and the team responsible for the reservation was kind enough to refund to my CC 100% of the charges within a business day.  Thank you for helping to fix the situation.

author: Tinks.
title: Choosing the RV was the easy bit
body: Choosing the RV was the easy bit. Paying for the rental and insurance with my UK international credit card was more difficult. Several attempts were made. Initially successful, the CC company AI decided that there was fraudulent activity and cancelled my card. I was informed by a phone call at 02.30 am in the middle of the dessert! Luckily the insurance payment had been accepted by another card- but we didn’t know that at that point. Not the best start! We had a fantastic time touring in Nevada- Lake Mead, Valley of Fire, Red Rocks and in Arizona- Willow Beach, Lake Havasu, Kingman, Grand Canyon, Lake Havasu Cattail Park and Route 66. The support from the RV staff and the owners was great. We were able to take our time and really get the feeling of the places we visited. We never rushed. Places we missed are on a list for the next time we visit the USA. We have lifetime memories!

author: Charlotte reed
title: DO NOT TTRUST THIS PLACE!
body:
We rented this RV in good faith that it was in working condition.  We drove 98 miles to our first destination.  Once we parked the carbon monoxide monitor went off immediately.  Also, the water hose was bent at the connector and water went everywhere on the outside.  We would have had to purchase a new water hose in order to use for the three days we had rented.  Also, the sewer connector did not have fitting to connect to the outside tank.  
The ac in the front part of the unit does not work on the passenger side well.
We could have dealt with other items, however, we did not feel comfortable sleeping with the carbon monoxide possible problem, so we turned around and drove home with windows down on unit to ensure our wellbeing.
We returned the unit the next morning.  No one was at the location, so we were informed to leave keys in box and park which we did.  I have video and pictures of all.
We requested at least a partial refund because I had paid for nights in RV parks we didn't get to use.  Instead Scott charged against yhe cc he had on file an additional charge because we did not file up with gas.
I have talked with an attorney and plan to take this to small claims court.
Sincerely
Charlotte Reed

author: Kevin Doglio
title: Punitive hidden fees
body: Everything went pretty well until RV Share charged a $9 administrative fee on the $60 charge for asking the owners to dump the tank I felt like I was pick pocketed at the airport. That's a 25% penalty for a common invoicing business practice. It's great for RV Share, more money for an automated process and absolutely no choice for me. It's disingenuous and RV Share should make it reasonable and not punitive. The owners were great. RV Share, not so much.

author: Kat Dom
title: Too many fees
body: It was a great experience with owners of the RV we rented. RV was exactly as described and they were great with communication.  I do feel that RV Share has way too many fees. For instance the RV was delivered to us, no one drove it or towed it anywhere yet we had to pay for drivers insurance. The fees were honestly the only thing that may stop us from using RV Share again.

author: Barbara
title: When I worked with representatives of…
body: When I worked with representatives of RVShare as I had some issues, they assisted me professionally and promptly.  May I suggest if the automated system does not provide you the answers (and it did for the most part) do not just quit but call the representatives; they will resolve your concerns.

author: Courtney Kerns
title: Always a great experience
body: I have rented from LensRentals many, many times and always had the best experience. The order is always on time and correct, and it’s a piece of cake to return the gear. The customer service is fantastic! It’s very easy to talk to someone on the phone or get email support from an actual person. I just had a major problem which was the fault of UPS, and they communicated extremely well as we resolved it. Don’t ever change that top-notch customer service! Highly recommend.
reply: NA

author: Agape Lataillade
title: I've been having major problems with…
body:
I've been having major problems with KitSplit ... a year ago I had a rental go bad, resulting a $10k+ claim, $8k+ of which they still owe me. It is impossible to reach anyone at the "company". No phone numbers exist. I email the customer service platform and might finally get a brief response after sending multiple messages for months, and my questions are always ignored.

I have never experienced such a lack of communication and information from a company before. 

It’s been 1 year now since the rental took place. I was told LAST MAY by Andi Avery & Stephanie Morris that I would received MONTHLY payments of $1k until paid in full. I received 1 payment, and then NOTHING until November. After reaching out dozens of more times, the co-founder, Ken Amarit, messaged me an sent 1 more "monthly" payment to me via Zelle in November. NOTHING in December, NOTHING in January, and no one will respond again. I HAVE ONLY RECEIVED 2 “monthly” payments since May. 

Kitsplit has been frustrating & unethical to say the very least. I won't stop reaching to them and calling out their illicit and crooked business practices until I'm paid what I am owed in full.
reply: NA

author: Ulla Törnroos
title: No substantive response to damage case since January
body:
I have been trying since January 2026 to obtain a substantive response from Getaround regarding a €3,300+ clutch and dual-mass flywheel repair after repeated rentals of my vehicle through the platform.
Despite multiple contacts via the app, phone support and email — including to the legal department — I have not received any response beyond automated replies and references to the standard damage compensation procedure, which does not apply in this case.
I have been renting out my car through Getaround for several years and therefore find this lack of response particularly disappointing, especially in light of the recent announcements regarding Getaround’s European expansion and strengthening of its user community.
I still hope the matter can be reviewed properly by a responsible person.
reply: NA
